content.query:
--Concepts we are learning in this usecase 
-- 1. Learning about usage of External Data Source in the OPENROWSET function
-- 2. Learning about usage of External Data Source, External File Format & External Table Concept

use wd36db;

--to list the database names
select name from sys.databases;


--How the table is accessed hierarchy in the Azure Sysnapse: dbname.schemaname.tablename
select top 10 * from wd36schema.dbo.claims_parquet;

-- 1. Learning about usage of External Data Source in the OPENROWSET function
---------------------------------------------------------
-- CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE
---------------------------------------------------------

USE wd36db;

--DROP EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE raw_container;
--Ext Data source for BLOB storage (Not preferred)
CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE datasource_blob
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'https://we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/container1/'
);

--Ext Data source for ADLS storage (preferred for performance)
CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE datasource_adls
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'abfss://container1@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/'
);


---------------------------------------------------------
-- READ CSV USING OPENROWSET function using EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE
---------------------------------------------------------
--Accessing BLOB data
SELECT *
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'synapsedata/custs',--Only folder and file we can just change, no need of using the fixed storage acct or container info everytime
    DATA_SOURCE = 'datasource_blob',--I don't require to give the complete uri of the ADLS/BLOB everytime in the openrowset
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = FALSE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) AS data1;

--Accessing ADLS data
SELECT *
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'synapsedata/custs',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'datasource_adls',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    HEADER_ROW = FALSE,
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) AS data1;


--Using openrowset With Header (equivalent to what in spark? schema(StructType(StructField))))
SELECT *
FROM OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'synapsedata/custs',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'datasource_adls',
    FORMAT = 'CSV',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
) 
WITH ( --schema(structtype(structfield....))
    CustomerID INT,
    FirstName VARCHAR(100),
    LastName VARCHAR(50),
    Age INT,
    Occupation VARCHAR(100)
) AS data1;


--2. Learning about usage of EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT + External Data Source + EXTERNAL TABLES

---------------------------------------------------------
-- CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE  to define the storage acct and container once for all
---------------------------------------------------------

CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE datasource_adls1
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'abfss://container1@we47toolstorage.dfs.core.windows.net/'
);

---------------------------------------------------------
-- CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT  to define the file format, delimiter, header etc., once for all
---------------------------------------------------------
--This Command defines how Azure Synapse should interpret the structure of a flat file

--DROP EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT synapse_csv_format;
CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT csv_comma_noheader_format
WITH
(
    FORMAT_TYPE = DELIMITEDTEXT,
    FORMAT_OPTIONS
    (
        FIELD_TERMINATOR = ',',--delimiter/sep in spark
        FIRST_ROW = 1 --Read the data from line 1 onwards (since we don't have header)
    )
);


---------------------------------------------------------
-- CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE once for all to allow our analytics users or Power BI dashboard, to access the underlying ADLS datalake data..
---------------------------------------------------------

CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE ext_cust_table
(
    CustomerID INT,
    FirstName VARCHAR(50),
    LastName VARCHAR(50),
    Age INT,
    Occupation VARCHAR(100)
)
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'synapsedata/custs',
    DATA_SOURCE = datasource_adls1,
    FILE_FORMAT = csv_comma_noheader_format
);

select * from ext_cust_table;--Behind the scene, structure come from metadata(sqlserver), data come from ADLS, compute come from Synapse Serverless


---------------------------------------------------------
-- CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE (with first row header): 
---------------------------------------------------------

CREATE EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT synapse_csv_format_header
WITH
(
    FORMAT_TYPE = DELIMITEDTEXT,
    FORMAT_OPTIONS
    (
        FIELD_TERMINATOR = ',',
        FIRST_ROW = 2 --Read the data from 2nd row onwards
    )
);

--drop external table ext_cust_tbl_header; --Very very important interview question - Drop will only drop the table structure but not the data
CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE ext_cust_tbl_header
(
    custid INT,
    fname VARCHAR(50),
    lname VARCHAR(50),
    age INT,
    prof VARCHAR(100)--inside the file, header has profession, i am overriding with prof
)
WITH
(
    LOCATION = 'synapsedata/custs_header',
    DATA_SOURCE = datasource_adls1,
    FILE_FORMAT = synapse_csv_format_header);

---------------------------------------------------------
-- QUERY EXTERNAL TABLE
---------------------------------------------------------

select * from ext_cust_tbl_header;

--Very very important interview question? CTE means what or have you used CTE queries?
--CTE - Common Table Expression 


--Inline View/From Clause Subquery (extracting data from ext_cust_tbl_header twice to reuse as t1 and t3)
--Not performant or good practice of writing query in general.
--select * from (some query here) as temptbl;

select t1.*,t2.* from 
(select * from ext_cust_tbl_header) t1
inner join (select * from ext_cust_tbl_header) t2
on t1.custid=t2.custid
union 
select t3.* from (select * from ext_cust_tbl_header) t3;

--Common table expression (extracting data from ext_cust_tbl_header only once and reuse as t1 multiple times in the given query)
--CTE
-- rather than writing like this (inline view), select * from (some query here) as temptbl;
-- write the query like this (CTE), with temptbl as (some query here) select * from temptl1;

with t1 as (select * from ext_cust_tbl_header),
t2 as (select * from ext_cust_tbl_header)
select t1.*,t2.* from t1 inner join t2 on t1.custid=t2.custid
union 
select t1.*,1 custid,'x' fname,'y' lname,1 age,'x' prof from t1
UNION
select t2.*,1 custid,'x' fname,'y' lname,1 age,'x' prof from t2;
